meta.name: The Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: An end-of-day deterministic reflection tool walking through three psychological axes.

nodes.text:
Good evening. You made it through another day — that's worth something.
This won't take long. No right answers. Just honest ones.
Before we dig in — how did today feel, overall?

options.label: Energizing — I was in the zone
Productive — got things done
Draining — it wore me down
Frustrating — things didn't go my way

nodes.text:
You said today felt energizing or productive.
When something went well, what do you think made it happen?

options.label: I prepared well for it
I adapted when the plan changed
The right people showed up at the right time
Timing just worked out in my favour

nodes.text:
You said today felt draining or frustrating.
When things got hard, where did your attention go first?

options.label: What I could still control in the situation
What went wrong and who was responsible
What I'd do differently next time
How stuck I felt

nodes.text: Was there a moment today where you made a call — even a small one — that changed how something went?

options.label: Yes, and I can name it
Maybe — but I'm not sure it mattered much
Not really — the situation didn't give me room
I don't think I had much say in how today went

nodes.text:
You stayed in the driver's seat today — even when the road changed.
That's not luck. Noticing your own hand in how things go is a habit, and it's one worth keeping.

Today felt like it happened *to* you more than *with* you. That's worth sitting with —
not to assign blame, but to look for the small choices that were still yours.
There's almost always at least one.

Today was a mix — some things you steered, some things steered you. That's honest.
Most days are exactly that. The question is whether you noticed the moments you had a choice.
Now let's shift. Not *how* you handled things — but *what* you brought to the people around you today.

Think about one specific interaction today — a meeting, a message, a hallway moment.
Which of these fits best?

options.label: I helped someone with something that wasn't my job
I did something extra without being asked
I felt my effort went unnoticed or unrewarded
I was frustrated others weren't pulling their weight

nodes.text: At the end of today, what thought comes most naturally to you?

options.label: Did I move things forward for the people depending on me?
Did I get credit for what I contributed?
Did I learn something that makes me more useful tomorrow?
Did I get what I needed to do my job well?

nodes.text:
You were thinking about what you gave today — not just what you got back.
Most people keep a mental ledger without realising it. You weren't tallying. That's a rare posture.

Today you were tracking what you gave versus what came back — recognition, credit, effort from others.
That's human. But it's worth asking: what would it feel like to give without keeping score?

Today was a push-pull — part of you was giving, part of you was watching to see if it was noticed.
That tension is normal. The fact that you're noticing it now is already something.
One last lens. Not what you did, not what you gave — but *who* was in your frame of view today.
When you picture today's biggest moment — good or bad — who else is in the frame?

options.label: Just me — it was mine to figure out
Me and one other person directly involved
My team — we were all in it together
Someone I don't usually think about — a user, customer, or someone downstream

nodes.text: Did anyone today have it harder than you — and did you notice?

options.label: Yes, and I did something about it
Yes, but I had my own things to deal with
I was heads-down — I'm not sure I looked up
Not that I noticed

nodes.text:
You looked up today. You saw someone else's world alongside yours — and you did something about it.
That's not a small thing. It's actually the whole thing.

You were thinking in terms of 'we' today, not just 'I'. That's a wider frame than most people operate from.
The team was real to you — not just a backdrop.

Today you were aware of others but pulled by your own current. That's honest —
you can only give what you have. The question is whether there's a little more in the tank than you think.

Today your world was close-in — your tasks, your stress, your goals. Sometimes that's necessary.
But widening the frame, even slightly, tends to make the weight feel different. Something to try tomorrow.
Here's how today looked.

summary_reflections.internal+giving+transcendent: You showed up fully today — owning your choices, giving without keeping score, and seeing beyond yourself. That's the full picture.
summary_reflections.internal+giving+wide: You handled today well, gave generously, and kept the team in view. Strong day.
summary_reflections.internal+giving+narrow: You steered well and gave genuinely — your circle of concern was a bit close, but the foundation is solid.
summary_reflections.internal+giving+self: You handled today well and gave genuinely — but your frame stayed close-in. Tomorrow, look up once.
summary_reflections.internal+entitlement+transcendent: You were in control today and saw beyond yourself — but part of you was tracking the scoreboard. Worth noticing.
summary_reflections.internal+entitlement+wide: You steered well today and thought about the team — but part of you was watching for credit. Worth noticing.
summary_reflections.internal+entitlement+narrow: You were in control today but in a private way — focused on your outcomes and your credit. Agency is powerful. The question is what you point it at.
summary_reflections.internal+entitlement+self: You were in control today but focused tightly — on your outcomes, your credit, your goals. The next step is pointing that energy outward.
summary_reflections.external+giving+transcendent: Today felt like it pushed you around, but you gave anyway and thought about people beyond yourself. That's resilience with generosity. Not easy.
summary_reflections.external+giving+wide: Today felt out of your hands, but you kept the team in view and gave what you could. That counts.
summary_reflections.external+giving+narrow: You gave today even when you felt powerless. That counts. Tomorrow, look for one small choice that was actually yours.
summary_reflections.external+giving+self: You gave today even when you felt powerless. That counts. Tomorrow, look for one small choice that was yours.
summary_reflections.external+entitlement+transcendent: Today had a push-pull quality — but you saw people beyond yourself. That wider view is the thing to hold onto.
summary_reflections.external+entitlement+wide: Today felt out of your hands, and you were watching to see if you'd get what you deserved. The team was in your frame though — that's real.
summary_reflections.external+entitlement+narrow: Today felt like it happened to you, and you were tallying closely. That's a tiring place to stand. See if tomorrow has one moment you can steer.
summary_reflections.external+entitlement+self: Today felt like it happened to you, and you were watching for what you'd get. That's a tiring place to stand. See if tomorrow has one moment you can steer.

closing_questions.internal: Where tomorrow will you have a choice that's easy to miss?
closing_questions.external: What's one thing tomorrow that's actually within your control — even if it feels small?

nodes.text: That's it for tonight. See you tomorrow.
